channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: A Fly Tipping on Street problem has been found, 21st July
item.description:
Durlston Road is full of rubbish! Please see pictures attached and organise collection

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Durlston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass opp 5 Ruxley Close needs to be cut, 21st July
item.description:
Please cut a grass opposite 5 Ruxley close , it’s not resolved
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ruxley Close, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Poppy Close car park flytipping, 21st July
item.description:
Someone has dumped 2 - 3 mattresses and 2- 3 bed frames. Please can this be removed as rats have been seen.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Poppy Close, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress, black bags and dangerous wood flytipped, 21st July
item.description:
Flytipping outside Robert Bell house which is being continued to be added to as the weeks go on. Rubbish includes old mattresses, black bin bags, and dangerous bits of wood. Please can this be sorted as soon as possible, the rubbish is growing by the day and will attract rodents. It is also unsafe and the housing block as several families with young children.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rouel Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 7 Montrell road, 21st July
item.description:
The building 7 Montrell Road has always got rubbish bins outside their household which overflows onto the streets and pavement. I find it difficult to walk on the pavement especially with my stroller and 18 month old daughter. The occupants of 7 Montrell road do not respect the correct items to put in recycling and for over a month their green bins have not collected which means that their waste does not fit into the bins and they are chucking the rest of their rubbish next to the bins in the road. It is also causing foxes to tear into black bags and leave more rubbish on the streets. It is a hazard and a health and safety risk.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Montrell Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Carpet dumped, 21st July
item.description:
Can you collect please?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree knocked down, 21st July

A tree was knocked down by a truck delivering a skip. It’s now blocking the footpath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Loxton Road, Perry Vale, Greater London

item.title: Black sack, 21st July
item.description:
A black sack has been flytipped on the grass area near number 6 there are alot of flies and magots around the bag, it has been there a couple of days now, could this be removed asap.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Strata Road, Northumberland Heath, Erith, Greater London

item.title: Rats, 21st July
item.description:
There used to be rats bait around the ponds, but they have been removed. Rats now looking for food in daylight hours.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockwell Park Row, Tulse Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, 21st July
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 21/07/26 09:35 Litter Lat/Long - 51.3875644, 0.0366896 what3words - plant.jungle.froze Nearest address - Flat 1, Regency Court, 14 Mackintosh Street, Bromley, BR2 9GL Plastic bottles and coffee cup Reference - 5740623 Map link - open Google map Report history - Status: Received 21/07/2026 09:34 Plastic bottles and coffee cup
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A21, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
